top_signals.title: Politics live: Burnham to become Prime Minister; City awaits new Chancellor
top_signals.summary: Welcome back to the City AM liveblog. It’s Monday and all eyes are on 10 Downing Street. Sir Keir Starmer will visit King Charles to formally resign as Prime Minister. Andy Burnham will then officially become his successor and deliver his first speech as Prime Minister. The market, which has long been trying to size [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Remarks by Commissioner Albuquerque at the press conference on the Report on the competitiveness of the EU banking sector

top_signals.title: Colombia's New Government Prepares Fossil Fuel Comeback
top_signals.summary: On 21st June, conservative, Trump-endorsed presidential candidate Abelardo de la Espriella won the election by just 1 per cent over Iván Cepeda, who was expected to take over the Socialist Party leadership from current President Gustavo Petro. He will be sworn in as president on 7th August, after which we can expect significant changes across the board. Petro, who was considered Colombia’s first leftist leader, brought about major change during his time in power, overhauling the country’s tax and health systems and supporting…
